tasks.title: TUI ergonomics: open task scope targets in $EDITOR and copy file:line references
evidence: `docs/tui-task-management.md` documents rich task navigation/editing, but there is no documented way to open a scoped file from the TUI in an external editor.
Tasks frequently include `scope` paths (and watch-mode includes `Detected in: file:line` notes), which are natural jump targets.
plan: Add a TUI action (keybinding + command palette entry) to open the selected task's primary scope path in `$EDITOR` (or `open`/`xdg-open` fallback) without blocking the UI.
Parse common file:line patterns from `notes` (e.g., `Detected in: path:line`) and provide a 'copy reference' action that writes `path:line` to clipboard (platform-gated) or prints it for manual copy.
Keep the minimal slice to local execution only (no network, no PR automation).
Update `docs/tui-task-management.md` with the new keybindings and examples.
Add tests around path selection/parsing (unit tests) and ensure the feature is disabled gracefully when `$EDITOR` is unset.
Verify by running `make ci` and manually smoke-testing `ralph tui` to open a file from a seeded task scope.

custom_fields.user_value: Fewer context switches: jump from task → code in one keystroke.

tasks.title: Add a read-only TUI view for parallel run-loop state (workers, PRs, merge blockers)
evidence: `docs/workflow.md` states parallel execution is CLI-only and persists state to `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json`.
Operators running `ralph run loop --parallel` lack an interactive UI to inspect state, failures, and merge blockers without opening the state JSON manually.
plan: Add a TUI screen/overlay to load and render `.ralph/cache/parallel/state.json` with a concise status table (in-flight tasks, PR URLs, merge blockers, last error).
Keep this view read-only as the minimal slice (no starting/stopping parallel runs from the TUI).
Add quick actions: open PR URL (shell) and copy PR URL; reload state from disk.
Add tests for state parsing/render model (unit tests) and ensure missing/invalid state files fail gracefully with guidance.
Update `docs/tui-task-management.md` with navigation to the parallel state view.
Verify by running `make ci` and manually simulating a state file to confirm rendering works end-to-end.

custom_fields.user_value: Makes parallel ops debuggable without leaving the TUI; reduces operator error.

tasks.title: Implement task time tracking and productivity analytics
evidence: Current `ralph queue stats` shows basic completion rates and tag breakdowns
No per-task time tracking or velocity metrics
`docs/productivity.md` exists but is not referenced in main docs
Users cannot track how long tasks take or identify bottlenecks
No sprint/iteration tracking or burndown velocity over time
plan: Add `started_at` and `completed_at` timestamps to task schema (if not present)
Create `ralph task start <TASK_ID>` command to mark task start time
Extend `ralph queue stats` with time-based metrics: avg task duration, velocity by tag
Add `ralph productivity` command with subcommands: `summary`, `velocity`, `bottlenecks`
Implement sprint tracking: `ralph productivity sprint start/end` with configurable duration
Add per-runner performance metrics to help users optimize runner selection
Create productivity dashboard in TUI showing trends and insights
Verify by running `ralph productivity summary` and checking metrics appear

custom_fields.measurable_impact: Track 20%+ improvement in task completion velocity after adoption
custom_fields.user_value: Enables data-driven workflow optimization; identifies slow runners/task types

tasks.title: Add batch task operations for efficient queue management
evidence: Current CLI requires individual task operations: `ralph task status doing RQ-0001`
No way to bulk-update multiple tasks at once via CLI
TUI supports some batch operations but CLI parity is incomplete
Users managing large queues need efficient bulk operations
`ralph task batch` exists but is limited to status/field edits only
plan: Extend `ralph task batch` with comprehensive operations: delete, archive, clone, split
Add filter-based batch operations: `--tag-filter`, `--status-filter`, `--scope-filter`
Implement `ralph task batch delete --status rejected --older-than 30d`
Add `ralph task batch clone --tag template` to clone all template tasks
Support batch plan updates: `ralph task batch edit plan --append 'New step'`
Add dry-run mode for all batch operations to preview changes
Create batch operation confirmation with summary: 'This will affect N tasks'
Verify by running batch operations with --dry-run and confirming expected changes

custom_fields.time_to_ship: incremental - add one batch operation at a time
custom_fields.user_value: Reduces queue maintenance time by 80% for large projects

tasks.title: Add integration testing framework for Ralph CLI
evidence: Current tests are primarily unit tests colocated with implementation
Integration tests exist but coverage is limited
No end-to-end CLI testing framework for command workflows
Testing complex multi-command scenarios requires manual execution
AGENTS.md emphasizes `make ci` but lacks integration test documentation
plan: Create `crates/ralph/tests/integration/` directory structure
Implement CLI test harness using `assert_cmd` and `predicates` crates
Add test fixtures for queue files, config, and project structures
Create integration tests for: init, task creation, queue operations, run workflow
Add mock runner for testing without external AI dependencies
Implement snapshot testing for CLI output consistency
Add test for parallel execution with mocked git operations
Verify by running `cargo test --workspace` and seeing new integration tests pass

custom_fields.user_value: Reduces regression risk; enables confident refactoring
custom_fields.measurable_impact: 80%+ integration test coverage for CLI commands

tasks.title: Create plugin system for custom task processors and runners
evidence: Current runners are hardcoded: codex, opencode, gemini, claude, cursor, kimi, pi
No way for users to add custom runners without modifying Ralph source
Task processing logic is tightly coupled to internal implementation
Users cannot add custom pre/post-processing hooks
No support for external task validators or custom completion criteria
plan: Design plugin trait interface for custom runners
Implement plugin discovery from `~/.config/ralph/plugins/` and `.ralph/plugins/`
Support WASM plugins for sandboxed custom processors
Add plugin configuration in config.json with enable/disable toggles
Create plugin API for: custom runners, task validators, completion hooks
Add `ralph plugin list` and `ralph plugin install <url>` commands
Document plugin development guide with examples
Verify by creating a sample plugin that logs task completions

custom_fields.user_value: Enables ecosystem growth; custom integrations without forks
custom_fields.differentiation: Unique capability among AI task runners

tasks.title: Add AI-powered task estimation and effort prediction
evidence: Current `eta_calculator.rs` exists but implementation is basic
No AI-powered estimation based on task content and scope
Users cannot predict completion times for queue
Historical task data is not used to improve future estimates
No integration between estimated and actual duration
plan: Extend task schema with `estimated_duration` and `actual_duration` fields
Implement ML-based estimation using task title, scope, tags, and historical data
Add `ralph task estimate <TASK_ID>` command using AI runner
Create estimation model that learns from completed tasks
Add queue-level ETA: `ralph queue eta` showing predicted completion
Integrate estimates into TUI showing expected duration
Add confidence intervals and uncertainty indicators
Verify by estimating 5 tasks and comparing to actual completion times

custom_fields.user_value: Improves planning accuracy; sets realistic expectations
custom_fields.measurable_impact: Estimates within 25% of actual duration after 20 tasks

tasks.title: Create interactive onboarding tutorial and guided setup
evidence: Current `ralph init` has basic wizard but limited guidance
Quick start guide exists but is static documentation
No interactive tutorial for first-time users
Complex features (parallel runs, phase overrides) lack guided introduction
Users learn by trial and error rather than structured guidance
plan: Create `ralph tutorial` command with interactive lessons
Design 5-7 tutorial modules: basics, task creation, running, TUI, advanced
Add hands-on exercises that create real tasks and execute them
Implement progress tracking and completion badges
Add contextual help tooltips in TUI for first-time users
Create video/gif demonstrations for complex workflows
Add 'tip of the day' feature for discovering features
Verify by running tutorial and completing all modules

custom_fields.measurable_impact: New user success rate >90% within 30 minutes
custom_fields.user_value: Reduces time-to-productivity from hours to minutes

tasks.title: Implement smart task suggestions from code analysis
evidence: Current `ralph watch` detects TODO/FIXME comments and creates tasks
No analysis of actual code to suggest improvements
No detection of code smells, anti-patterns, or technical debt
Missing integration with static analysis tools (clippy, cargo-audit)
Users must manually identify refactoring opportunities
plan: Extend `ralph watch` with code analysis integration
Add detection patterns: dead code, complex functions, missing tests
Integrate with clippy, cargo-audit, cargo-outdated for actionable tasks
Create `ralph suggest` command for one-time codebase analysis
Add AI-powered code review suggestions as task candidates
Implement confidence scoring for suggestions (high/medium/low)
Add one-click task creation from suggestions in TUI
Verify by running `ralph suggest` and checking relevant tasks are proposed

custom_fields.user_value: Proactively identifies improvement opportunities; reduces manual scanning

tasks.title: Implement configurable notification and webhook system
evidence: Current notification system is basic with desktop notifications only
`agent.notification` config exists but limited to on/off toggles
No webhook support for external integrations
Users cannot receive notifications on mobile or via Slack/Discord
No custom notification rules based on task properties
plan: Extend notification system with webhook support (Slack, Discord, Teams)
Add `ralph notify config` command for easy setup
Implement notification templates with variable substitution
Add conditional notifications: by priority, tag, or status
Create mobile push notification via webhook bridge
Add notification batching to reduce spam
Implement quiet hours and do-not-disturb mode
Verify by configuring Slack webhook and confirming notifications arrive

custom_fields.user_value: Enables remote monitoring; integrates with existing workflows
custom_fields.time_to_ship: incremental - add one channel at a time

tasks.title: Add performance profiling and optimization insights
evidence: No performance profiling of Ralph itself
Users cannot identify slow operations or bottlenecks
No metrics on queue operations, git operations, or runner startup
Large queues may have unknown performance issues
No optimization suggestions based on usage patterns
plan: Add `--profile` flag to enable performance tracing
Implement timing instrumentation for all major operations
Create `ralph doctor --performance` to diagnose bottlenecks
Add queue performance metrics: load time, search speed, validation time
Generate performance reports: `ralph report performance`
Add optimization suggestions based on queue size and structure
Implement caching for expensive operations (dependency graph, validation)
Verify by running with --profile and checking performance data is collected

custom_fields.measurable_impact: 50%+ improvement in queue operations for large projects
